narrative: The SARS-CoV-2 spike protein receptor-binding domain (RBD, residues 319-541) mediates host cell entry by binding human ACE2. The RBD undergoes conformational changes between 'up' (ACE2-accessible) and 'down' (hidden) states. Key contact residues (K417, E484, N501) are hotspots for immune-evasive mutations in variants of concern (Alpha, Beta, Delta, Omicron).

drugs.name: Sotrovimab
drugs.mechanism: Monoclonal antibody binding conserved RBD epitope

key_findings: Cryo-EM reveals RBD conformational dynamics between up/down states on trimeric spike
N501Y mutation increases ACE2 binding affinity 10-fold, driving Alpha variant spread
E484K enables immune escape from convalescent and vaccine-elicited antibodies
Pan-sarbecovirus antibodies target conserved RBD class 4 epitopes

suggested_experiments: SPR to measure RBD-ACE2 binding kinetics for variant mutations
Pseudovirus neutralization assay with convalescent sera
Cryo-EM of spike trimer to visualize RBD dynamics
